mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward 20-day returns show no significant negative correlation with rate increases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth outpaces consumer price inflation, corporate revenue expansion exceeds cost pressures, supporting profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS growth and CPIAUCSL growth yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling windows, or forward returns are not significantly positive when sales growth exceeds inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production during an economic expansion cycle reflects strong manufacturing demand and capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns during expansion periods show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO growth, or the hypothesis fails to outperform a baseline model in regime-filtered walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which rapidly compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for CPIAUCSL remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows, or 5-day forward returns show no significant negative relationship with inflation readings.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls growth exceeding 200,000 jobs per month indicates robust labor demand and consumer income growth, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in > 55% of months where payrolls exceed 200k, or the threshold signal yields a Rank IC below 0.03 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts combined with high federal funds rates signal a credit-constrained construction sector and weakening consumer balance sheets, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling HOUST and high FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with Rank IC below 0.02 or positive returns occurring in > 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects peak economic activity and corporate earnings momentum, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns during late-cycle periods show no significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth, or the regime-conditional signal underperforms a flat baseline in walk-forward validation.
